articles.title: Madonna, Shakira & BTS to Headline First-Ever World Cup Halftime Show
articles.summary: FIFA has confirmed Madonna, Shakira, and BTS as headliners for the first-ever FIFA World Cup Final halftime show, set for July 19 at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey. The show, curated by Coldplay's Chris Martin, will clock in at 11 minutes and raise funds for the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund. The announcement was made in a wildly fun social video featuring Sesame Street and Muppets characters alongside a FaceTime cameo from BTS themselves.
articles.content:
The FIFA World Cup Final just got a whole lot bigger. For the first time in history, the FIFA World Cup Final will feature a halftime show, and FIFA is going all out, with BTS, Madonna, and Shakira set to headline the festivities from New Jersey's MetLife Stadium on July 19. Think of it as the Super Bowl halftime show, but for the sport that literally the entire planet watches.

The show will clock in at 11 minutes, and it will be curated by Chris Martin, the lead singer of Coldplay, who will play a key role in the artistic direction of the halftime production. The announcement itself came via a brilliantly chaotic video. The news was announced in a social video starring Coldplay's Chris Martin alongside Sesame Street's Elmo and Cookie Monster, as well as Kermit, Miss Piggy and more from The Muppets, with the unlikely crew even calling up BTS on FaceTime. It is exactly as unhinged and wonderful as it sounds.

All three acts are arriving at this moment with serious momentum. BTS made a major return to music earlier this year with the March 20 release of their sixth studio album ARIRANG, the South Korean group's first project since they went on hiatus for each member to complete their military service. Madonna is also gearing up for a busy July, with her Confessions II album, the sequel to her 2005 classic Confessions on a Dance Floor, arriving July 3. And Shakira is no stranger to the World Cup stage at all. Shakira has previously produced Waka Waka (This Time for Africa) and La La La for the tournaments in 2010 and 2014. Now she also has a new official World Cup anthem ready: her official 2026 World Cup song "Dai Dai" was made with Burna Boy.

While this marks the first time the World Cup Final has had a halftime show, two of the July performers have previously headlined the Super Bowl halftime show. Madonna took center field at the 2012 Super Bowl, while Shakira co-headlined with Jennifer Lopez in 2020. The show also has a bigger purpose behind it. The show will raise funds for the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund, dedicated to improving access to quality education and football for children around the world. July 19 cannot come fast enough.
keyPoints: Madonna, Shakira, and BTS will headline the first-ever FIFA World Cup Final halftime show on July 19 at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey
The show will be 11 minutes long and curated by Coldplay's Chris Martin
The announcement was made via a video featuring Sesame Street and Muppets characters, with a FaceTime cameo from BTS
BTS recently released their comeback album ARIRANG, Madonna drops Confessions II on July 3, and Shakira has the new official World Cup song 'Dai Dai' with Burna Boy
The show will raise funds for the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund

articles.title: Sam Altman Forced to Answer: Are You a Liar?
articles.summary: Sam Altman took the witness stand in the Musk v. Altman trial in Oakland, California, where Elon Musk's attorney grilled him on accusations of dishonesty from former colleagues, board members, and business associates. The trial, now in its third week, centers on Musk's claim that Altman and OpenAI president Greg Brockman 'stole a charity' by converting OpenAI from a nonprofit into a for-profit venture. When pressed on whether he always told the truth, Altman replied: 'I'm sure there are some times in my life when I did not.'
articles.content:
Sam Altman sat in a federal courthouse in Oakland, California on Tuesday and faced one of the most uncomfortable questions of his career. Elon Musk's attorney Steven Molo opened his cross-examination with a single pointed question: 'Are you completely trustworthy?' It was a blunt opening shot in what became a four-hour grilling of the OpenAI CEO.

Musk's lawyer Steven Molo cross-examined Altman and tried to paint the OpenAI chief as unreliable and dishonest, opening his line of questioning by asking Altman whether he is 'completely trustworthy.' Molo asked about concerns about honesty from several people Altman has interacted with, including employees of startup Loopt, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei, and former OpenAI board members Tasha MacCauley and Sue Yoon. Molo said Amodei had accused Altman of misrepresenting the terms of an investment to him, to which Altman testified: 'Dario has accused me of many things.'

The honesty attacks did not stop there. OpenAI cofounder Ilya Sutskever, who played a major role in Altman's displacement, testified on Monday that he spent months gathering evidence showing what he said was Altman's pattern of deception and poor management. Former board member Helen Toner said 'a phrase we used was a pattern of behavior, so no one single cause,' and that the pattern related to 'his honesty and candor, his resistance of board oversight.' Altman pushed back firmly. 'I believe I am an honest and trustworthy businessperson,' Altman said. And when asked if he had been called a liar by business associates, Altman said: 'I have heard people say that.'

Altman also went on the offensive against Musk. He said that Musk, who co-founded OpenAI alongside him in 2015, did not keep his promises and eventually deserted the young startup as it was trying to chart out an uncertain future. 'We were kind of left for dead,' Altman testified. Altman claimed Musk said he deserved 90% of the equity in OpenAI and that he mused about turning the company over to his children if he died without a successor plan in place. Altman called that a 'hair-raising moment' in OpenAI's early days.

If the United States District Court for the Northern District of California finds Altman, Brockman, and Microsoft liable for Musk's two civil claims of 'breach of charitable trust' and 'unjust enrichment,' Musk has asked for them to 'disgorge' up to $150 billion to the nonprofit entity. Closing arguments are on Thursday, and a decision from an advisory jury and the judge overseeing the case, Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers, are possible next week. The stakes could not be higher. The outcome of the trial could upend OpenAI's race toward an IPO at a valuation approaching $1 trillion.
keyPoints: Musk's lawyer Steven Molo opened cross-examination of Sam Altman by asking 'Are you completely trustworthy?'
Multiple former colleagues including Ilya Sutskever and Helen Toner testified about what they called Altman's pattern of dishonesty
Altman countered that Musk demanded 90% of OpenAI's equity and 'left us for dead'
Musk is seeking up to $150 billion to be returned to OpenAI's nonprofit arm
Closing arguments land Thursday, with a verdict possible as early as next week

articles.title: Alex Murdaugh Conviction Overturned Because Clerk Wanted Better Book Sales
articles.summary: The South Carolina Supreme Court unanimously overturned Alex Murdaugh's double murder convictions on Wednesday, ordering a new trial after ruling that court clerk Becky Hill improperly influenced the jury. Hill allegedly told jurors to watch Murdaugh's body language and suggested the deliberations should not take long, all while writing a book about the case. Murdaugh will stay in prison on separate financial crimes charges while prosecutors prepare to retry him.
articles.content:
Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions have been thrown out, and the reason is one of the more stunning details to come out of an already extraordinary case. The South Carolina Supreme Court ruled unanimously on Wednesday that Colleton County Clerk of Court Becky Hill improperly influenced the jury during Murdaugh's 2023 murder trial, and that her conduct denied him his right to a fair trial.

Here is what Hill allegedly did. During the six-week trial, Hill made comments to jurors that the court found crossed a serious line. One juror, referred to as Juror Z, said Hill told them to "watch his actions" and "watch him closely" when Murdaugh took the stand. That same juror said Hill told the jury "not to be fooled" by the defence's evidence, and said "this shouldn't take us long" when deliberations began. The juror told the court those comments made it seem like Murdaugh was already guilty before anyone had deliberated. Hill has since pleaded guilty to criminal charges connected to her conduct in the case.

The motive the defence alleged is remarkable. Murdaugh's legal team argued Hill was motivated by book sales. She was writing a tell-all called Behind the Doors of Justice: The Murdaugh Murders during and after the trial. A guilty verdict, they argued, would make for a better book. The justices were not subtle about this in their ruling, writing that Hill "was quite busy behind the doors of justice, thwarting the integrity of the justice system she was sworn to protect." The book was later pulled from publication after plagiarism allegations surfaced.

For context, Alex Murdaugh comes from one of South Carolina's most prominent legal dynasties. His father, grandfather, and great-grandfather served as the local prosecutor consecutively from 1920 to 2006. He was convicted in March 2023 of shooting his wife Maggie and their 22-year-old son Paul at their estate in June 2021. Prosecutors argued he carried out the killings to distract from the financial crimes that were about to destroy his reputation. In a 5-0 decision, the Supreme Court vacated his two life sentences for the murder charges.

Murdaugh is not walking free. He pleaded guilty separately to stealing around $12 million from his clients and is currently serving a 40-year federal sentence for those financial crimes. South Carolina Attorney General Alan Wilson said his office will retry Murdaugh on the murder charges as soon as possible. Murdaugh's legal team celebrated the ruling, saying they look forward to a new trial. The case has already spawned documentaries, podcasts, and books, and a retrial will bring the whole saga roaring back into the spotlight.
keyPoints: The South Carolina Supreme Court unanimously overturned Alex Murdaugh's murder convictions on 13 May 2026
Court clerk Becky Hill allegedly told jurors to watch Murdaugh's body language and implied guilt before deliberations
The defence argued Hill was motivated by wanting to sell more copies of her book about the trial
Murdaugh remains in prison serving a 40-year federal sentence for stealing $12 million from clients
South Carolina Attorney General Alan Wilson has vowed to retry Murdaugh on the murder charges

articles.title: Musk Says Nolan Cast Lupita Nyong'o as Helen of Troy to Win Awards
articles.summary: Elon Musk is back on X attacking Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey, claiming Nolan cast Lupita Nyong'o as Helen of Troy purely to chase Oscars. A new Time magazine profile confirmed not only Nyong'o's casting but a surprise second role as Helen's sister Clytemnestra. Critics are pointing out that Musk's Oscar-quota argument doesn't hold up and that Nolan's previous film Oppenheimer won seven Oscars with an all-white cast.
articles.content:
Elon Musk is once again firing at Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey, and this time the casting is officially confirmed. A Time magazine profile of Nolan published May 12 locked in what had previously been a rumor: Lupita Nyong'o is playing Helen of Troy in the $250 million epic. The profile also revealed a surprise second role. Nyong'o will also play Helen's sister Clytemnestra, wife of Agamemnon. Musk's response was swift.

Posting on X, Musk replied to a user asking why Nolan would change the race of characters with two words: "He wants the awards." The argument was that Nolan cast Nyong'o specifically to satisfy the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences' representation and inclusion standards, which were introduced in 2020 and went into effect for best picture eligibility starting in 2024. Conservative commentator Matt Walsh had posted that Nolan would have been "called racist" if he gave the role to a white actress, and Musk replied "True." Musk also amplified posts mocking Elliot Page, who is rumored to play Achilles in the film, calling the idea "one of the dumbest and twisted things I've ever heard."

The problem with the awards-quota argument is that it falls apart fast. To qualify for Oscar best picture consideration under the Academy's standards, a film only needs to meet two out of several criteria. Those criteria include subject matter, leadership, crew makeup, and audience development. They are not exclusively about casting. As Variety pointed out, Nolan's own Oppenheimer had an all-white cast and still took home seven Oscars including Best Picture and Best Director in 2024. The rules Musk described simply do not work the way he claimed.

Whoopi Goldberg and her co-hosts on The View weighed in, with the EGOT winner responding directly to those questioning Nyong'o's beauty. "I don't know if you realize this, Lupita is also considered one of the world's most beautiful women," Goldberg said. Nolan has not publicly responded to Musk's comments. The Odyssey, which also stars Tom Holland, Zendaya, Matt Damon, Anne Hathaway, and Charlize Theron among many others, is the first film shot entirely with IMAX cameras. It hits theaters July 17.

This is not the first round. Back in February, when Nyong'o's casting was still a rumor, Musk posted that "Chris Nolan has lost his integrity." The new wave of posts shows the controversy has only grown now that her dual role is confirmed. Whether any of this online noise translates to real box office impact for a summer blockbuster this stacked remains to be seen.
keyPoints: Elon Musk posted on X that Nolan cast Lupita Nyong'o as Helen of Troy to chase Oscar awards
A Time magazine profile confirmed Nyong'o plays both Helen of Troy and her sister Clytemnestra in The Odyssey
Musk also amplified attacks on Elliot Page, who is rumored to play Achilles
Critics note Musk's Oscar-quota argument is inaccurate — Nolan's Oppenheimer won 7 Oscars with an all-white cast
The Odyssey, the first film shot entirely on IMAX cameras, opens July 17

articles.title: Ubisoft Buried $500K in the Caribbean for Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced
articles.summary: Ubisoft has literally buried a $500,000 treasure chest somewhere in the Caribbean to promote Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced, and you can pay $40 to try and find it. The hunt, called Gold and Crystal: The Lost Treasure of Edward Kenway, involves solving 15 puzzles and could take up to five years to crack. The prize itself is a handcrafted crystal skull and a chest full of gold coins.
articles.content:
Ubisoft has gone full pirate. To celebrate the release of Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced, the company has partnered with French treasure hunt specialists Unsolved Hunts to create Gold and Crystal: The Lost Treasure of Edward Kenway. There is a real chest, buried somewhere in the Caribbean, containing treasure valued at $500,000.

Here is how it works. Gold and Crystal combines a crypted message in a bottle, a map of the Caribbean, and a collection of letters and archives containing 15 puzzles packed with Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced references. The puzzles are entirely remote and can be solved from anywhere in the world, without prior knowledge of the game. The first player who registers that they have actually found the X that marks the spot will be flown out to the Gulf of Mexico to uncover the spoils for themselves.

So what is in the chest? The actual treasure is sitting in a handcrafted replica of the Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced chest and features minted gold coins worth $350,000 and a crystal skull with a red opal jewel embedded in it worth $150,000. To get started, you need to pay for the Deckhand pack at $39.99, which comes with a digital version of the treasure hunt game including clues, rules, and more. You can pay up to $230 to get extra goodies, like a notebook and a board game.

Do not expect a quick win though. This hunt is designed to last between two and five years. Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced was officially announced last month and will release on PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and PC on July 9. The original Assassin's Creed Black Flag launched back in 2013 and followed pirate Edward Kenway sailing the Caribbean during the Golden Age of Piracy. It became one of the most beloved entries in the series. The full hunt will become available on November 9, so there is time to get your sea legs before the real puzzle begins.
keyPoints: Ubisoft buried a real $500,000 treasure chest somewhere in the Caribbean
The hunt is called Gold and Crystal: The Lost Treasure of Edward Kenway
Players must solve 15 puzzles to find the location, starting from $39.99 to enter
The prize includes gold coins worth $350,000 and a crystal skull with a red opal worth $150,000
The hunt officially begins November 9, 2026 and could last up to five years

articles.title: Claude AI Found a Bug That Unlocked a Man's $400K Bitcoin Wallet
articles.summary: X user Cprkrn went viral on May 13 after claiming Anthropic's Claude AI helped him recover 5 Bitcoin worth roughly $400,000, locked in a wallet since his college days after he changed the password while high and forgot it. Claude did not crack Bitcoin's encryption. Instead, it dug through old files, found a December 2019 wallet backup, spotted a bug in a recovery tool, and fixed it. The post drew over 414,000 views within hours.
articles.content:
Back in college, a guy known on X as Cprkrn bought 5 Bitcoin when each coin was worth about $250. He changed his wallet password at some point while intoxicated and promptly forgot what the new one was. That was roughly 11 years ago. Those 5 Bitcoin sat completely untouched and inaccessible, quietly growing in value while he could do nothing but watch.

Fast forward to May 13, 2026. Bitcoin is trading at around $79,600, making those 5 coins worth roughly $398,000. Cprkrn was not about to give up. He had already tried what he described as approximately 7 trillion password combinations using brute force tools. He hired multiple recovery services. He searched through old notes. Nothing worked. As a last resort, he dumped the entire contents of his old college computer, including files, notebooks, and backups, into Anthropic's Claude AI.

What Claude found was not a magic password crack. It found an older wallet backup file from December 2019 that predated the password change. It also spotted a bug in BTCRecover, a widely used open source Bitcoin wallet recovery tool. The bug was causing the shared key and the password to be combined in the wrong order during decryption. Claude identified the error, corrected the logic, ran the fixed process, and extracted the private keys. The terminal output Cprkrn screenshotted and posted to X read: "PRIVATE KEYS DECRYPTED! WE GOT IT!!! THE 5 BTC IS YOURS!"

The post exploded. It pulled in over 414,000 views and around 1,900 likes within hours, with responses from notable names across the crypto community including Nic Carter, Jesse Pollak, and Laura Shin. Cprkrn publicly thanked Anthropic and CEO Dario Amodei, joking that he would name his child after him. Some users raised security concerns about feeding wallet files into an AI, though experts quickly clarified that Claude did not bypass Bitcoin's cryptography. It simply acted as a very smart file archaeologist, connecting dots the owner already had but could not piece together himself. His advice to anyone else in the same boat? "Just mega dump all of your computers and notebooks into Claude."
keyPoints: Cprkrn recovered 5 Bitcoin worth roughly $400,000 using Anthropic's Claude AI on May 13
The wallet had been locked for about 11 years after he changed the password while high in college
Claude found a December 2019 wallet backup and fixed a bug in the BTCRecover recovery tool
Claude did not break Bitcoin encryption. It matched existing data and corrected a recovery workflow error
The post went viral with over 414,000 views and responses from major crypto figures

articles.title: Netflix Is Taking KPop Demon Hunters on a World Tour in 2027
articles.summary: Netflix and AEG Presents announced a global KPop Demon Hunters concert tour at Netflix's Upfront 2026 event in New York City on May 13. The tour will bring the two-time Oscar-winning animated film's music and characters to arenas worldwide in 2027. Fans can already join a waitlist for ticket updates as city and date details are still being finalized.
articles.content:
If you thought KPop Demon Hunters was done taking over the world, think again. Netflix and AEG Presents just announced a full global concert tour based on the blockbuster animated film, set to hit arenas worldwide in 2027. The news dropped during Netflix's Upfront 2026 presentation in New York City on Wednesday, May 13.

Netflix president of ad sales Amy Reinhard put the film's cultural footprint in plain terms at the presentation, pointing to sing-alongs, Halloween costumes, shoutouts at the U.S. Open and Buckingham Palace, and even a 25% spike in flights booked to Korea. That is not just a movie doing well. That is a full-blown cultural moment.

The tour promises "a live experience that will bring elements of the two-time Oscar-winning film to life in spectacular ways." Details are still sparse at this point, and Netflix has not confirmed whether any of the film's singing talent will appear, including EJAE, Audrey Nuna, and Rei Ami, who voiced the fictional K-pop girl group HUNTR/X, or the Saja Boys. Fans can join a waitlist right now to receive updates on cities, dates, and ticket details as announcements roll out.

At the 2026 Oscars, KPop Demon Hunters took home Best Animated Feature and Best Original Song for "Golden," making it the first K-pop song ever to win in that category. "Golden" also made history at the Grammys, winning Best Song Written for Visual Media and becoming the first K-pop track to earn that honor. So this tour is not just chasing hype. It is building on something that has already made genuine music history.

AEG Presents brings serious K-pop tour experience to the table, having previously handled global runs for Blackpink, Jennie, ATEEZ, Enhypen, G-Dragon, Le Sserafim, Big Bang, NCT 127, and Tomorrow x Together. A KPop Demon Hunters sequel film is also in the works. Netflix closed deals with directors Maggie Kang and Chris Appelhans for a follow-up as part of a new multiyear writing and directing pact, though a confirmed release date has not yet been announced. The concert tour and the sequel together make one thing very clear: Netflix is treating KPop Demon Hunters as a long-term franchise, not a one-hit wonder.
keyPoints: Netflix and AEG Presents announced a KPop Demon Hunters global concert tour set for 2027
The announcement was made at Netflix's Upfront 2026 event in New York City on May 13
The film won two Oscars including Best Original Song for "Golden", the first K-pop song to win that award
No tour cities, dates, or confirmed performers have been announced yet — fans can join a waitlist
A KPop Demon Hunters sequel film is also in development with directors Maggie Kang and Chris Appelhans

articles.title: Moscow Bans Nearly Everyone From Posting About Ukraine Drone Damage
articles.summary: Moscow has introduced a sweeping ban that prevents almost all citizens, businesses, government agencies, and emergency services from posting on social media about damage caused by Ukrainian drone strikes. Only the Russian defense ministry and the Moscow mayor's office are permitted to publish information about the attacks. The move is a dramatic attempt to control the public narrative around a war that is increasingly visible on Russian streets.
articles.content:
Russia is tightening its grip on information about the war, and now it is doing it in real time. Moscow has introduced a blanket ban that stops almost everyone in the city from posting about damage caused by Ukrainian drone strikes on social media. The scope of the ban is striking: it does not just apply to ordinary citizens or journalists.

The restrictions extend even to government agencies and emergency services, with only the Russian defense ministry and the Moscow mayor's office allowed to make posts about drone strike damage. That means fire brigades, municipal workers, and local officials responding to attacks cannot post what they are seeing, even as they deal with the aftermath on the ground.

This is a significant escalation in Russia's information crackdown. Ukraine has been stepping up drone attacks on Moscow and the surrounding region, with strikes increasingly reaching the Russian capital and creating visible, hard-to-hide evidence of the conflict. By locking down who can speak about this damage, Moscow is trying to control not just what Russians at home see, but also what gets out to international audiences watching Russian social media.

The ban reflects a broader pattern of Russia attempting to keep the domestic public narrative tightly managed throughout the war. Previous measures have included criminalizing the use of words like "war" and "invasion" to describe what the Kremlin calls a "special military operation," and blocking access to independent news websites. This latest move shows those efforts are intensifying as drone strikes become harder to ignore. Concentrating the right to post in just two official voices, the defense ministry and the mayor's office, gives Moscow maximum control over tone, timing, and scale of any damage acknowledgment.
keyPoints: Moscow has banned almost all residents, businesses, and government bodies from posting about Ukrainian drone strike damage on social media
The ban even covers emergency services and government agencies responding to attacks
Only the Russian defense ministry and the Moscow mayor's office are permitted to post about drone damage
Ukraine has been escalating drone strikes on Moscow and the surrounding region
The move is part of a wider Russian crackdown on information about the war that includes banning use of the word 'war' itself

articles.title: Florence Pugh's East of Eden Hits Netflix This Fall
articles.summary: Netflix has confirmed that East of Eden, Zoe Kazan's modern seven-episode adaptation of John Steinbeck's classic 1952 novel, will arrive in fall 2026. The announcement came with the release of the first teaser trailer during Netflix's upfront presentation on May 13. The series stars Florence Pugh as antihero Cathy Ames alongside Christopher Abbott, Mike Faist, and a deep ensemble cast.
articles.content:
Nearly four years in the making, East of Eden is finally coming. Zoe Kazan's adaptation of John Steinbeck's classic 1952 novel will arrive on Netflix later this year, with the first teaser unveiled during Netflix's upfront presentation after the limited series starring Florence Pugh was first announced almost four years ago. All seven episodes will debut simultaneously on Netflix in fall 2026.

East of Eden is a modern interpretation of Steinbeck's classic novel and will explore the multigenerational saga of the Trask family, focusing new attention on its indelible antihero, Cathy Ames, played by Pugh. The series spans decades in the lives of Cathy and the Trask family, and is bookended by the American Civil War and World War I. Netflix describes it as "a portrait of one family, but also of a society that is rapidly changing, in which men and women must navigate difficult decisions."

The series also stars Tracy Letts as Cyrus Trask, Martha Plimpton as Faye, Ciarán Hinds as Samuel Hamilton, Joseph Zada as Cal Trask, Joe Anders as Aron Trask, Christopher Abbott as Adam Trask, Mike Faist as Charles Trask, and Hoon Lee as Lee. Behind the camera, Garth Davis, best known for the Oscar-nominated film Lion, helmed episodes one through four, while Laure de Clermont-Tonnerre stepped in to direct episodes five through seven.

The project carries some remarkable family history. Zoe Kazan's grandfather, Elia Kazan, directed the legendary 1955 East of Eden film starring James Dean. The role of Cathy Ames, now taken by Pugh, was originally played by Jo Van Fleet, who won a Best Supporting Actress Oscar for the part. Zoe Kazan says the personal connection is not lost on her. "Working on this has been the greatest joy, and I am thrilled by the cast and crew we have assembled," Kazan told Netflix's Tudum.

The fall 2026 release date means the latter half of the year will be very busy for Florence Pugh, who is also set to star in Dune: Part Three and Avengers: Doomsday, both opening on December 18, 2026. East of Eden looks like the prestige TV event of the season, and based on this teaser, it has every reason to be.
keyPoints: East of Eden arrives on Netflix in fall 2026, with all seven episodes dropping at once
Florence Pugh plays antihero Cathy Ames in Zoe Kazan's modern adaptation of Steinbeck's 1952 novel
The cast also includes Christopher Abbott, Mike Faist, Ciarán Hinds, Martha Plimpton, and Tracy Letts
Zoe Kazan's grandfather Elia Kazan directed the iconic 1955 film version starring James Dean
Garth Davis directed episodes 1–4 and Laure de Clermont-Tonnerre directed episodes 5–7

articles.title: Trump Claims a Drug Brings Dead People Back to Life
articles.summary: At a press event on May 11, Donald Trump claimed that an unnamed new drug can bring people back from the dead, describing a patient who had last rites read to them and then recovered after being given the drug. Trump did not name the drug or provide any evidence for the claim. The remark landed in the context of the Trump administration's recent push around experimental psychedelic treatments including ibogaine.
articles.content:
On Monday May 11, at what was described as a routine press event, President Donald Trump said something that would have dominated headlines in any other era of American politics. He claimed a new drug can bring dead people back to life.

His exact words: "We've taken people that were dead. We had a person given the last rites — gone, the kids are crying and everything — and started them on this drug. And the person became better. It works." Trump did not name the drug, cite a study, point to a doctor, or provide any other context for the claim. No follow-up clarification was offered by the White House.

The comments came weeks after Trump signed an executive order in April accelerating research into psychedelic treatments, with a specific focus on ibogaine, a naturally occurring compound used in some countries to treat PTSD, addiction, depression, and anxiety. That order directed the FDA and the DEA to establish pathways for eligible patients to access investigational psychedelic drugs. It also extended Commissioner's National Priority Vouchers to psychedelic drugs that had received Breakthrough Therapy designations from the FDA. While that is a genuinely significant policy development, it is a far cry from resurrecting the dead.

The comment spread quickly online, with many questioning whether Trump was describing a remarkable and completely undocumented medical miracle or simply had no clear grasp of what the drug in question actually does. Futurism, which first picked up the clip, noted that exaggerated and medically unsupported claims have become a recurring feature of Trump's public appearances across both of his terms. No medical body or government agency has corroborated anything resembling a drug that reverses death.
keyPoints: Trump claimed at a May 11 press event that an unnamed new drug brings dead people back to life
He described a patient who received last rites and then recovered after being given the drug
No drug was named and no evidence or medical source was cited
The remarks came after Trump signed an April executive order accelerating research into psychedelic treatments including ibogaine
No medical body has corroborated a drug capable of reversing death

articles.title: World's First Pokémon Airport Opens With Giant Pikachu Displays
articles.summary: The world's first dedicated Pokémon airport is officially opening, bringing giant Pikachu displays, hidden Flying-type Pokémon, themed food, and exclusive merchandise passengers cannot find anywhere else. It marks a first in travel history for the Pokémon franchise, which turned 30 this year. If you needed a reason to book a flight, this might just be it.
articles.content:
Pokémon has taken over planes, theme parks, and entire city streets. Now it is taking over an entire airport. The world's first Pokémon-themed airport is opening its doors, and it is exactly as extra as you would hope. Giant Pikachu displays, airport-exclusive merchandise, themed food, and hidden Flying-type Pokémon scattered throughout the terminal are all part of the experience.

The hidden Flying-types are a fun detail worth calling out. Just like spotting Pokémon in the wild, travelers will need to keep their eyes open around the terminal to find them all. It turns a layover into a little adventure, which is honestly not something airports usually offer.

The timing is no accident. 2026 marks Pokémon's 30th anniversary, a milestone The Pokémon Company has been celebrating in a big way all year. From new Pokémon Jets in the skies to PokéPark Kanto opening at Yomiuriland in Tokyo, the franchise has been leaning hard into real-world experiences. A fully themed airport is the boldest move yet.

The exclusive merch angle is also a big deal for collectors. Airport-exclusive Pokémon items have always been some of the most sought-after in the fandom. A whole airport built around that concept means a lot of things you simply cannot get anywhere else. Whether you are a hardcore trainer or just someone who loves Pikachu, this is the kind of travel experience that turns an ordinary trip into a story worth telling.
keyPoints: The world's first Pokémon-themed airport is now opening
Giant Pikachu displays greet travelers throughout the terminal
Hidden Flying-type Pokémon are scattered around for fans to find
Themed food and airport-exclusive Pokémon merchandise are available
The opening coincides with Pokémon's 30th anniversary in 2026

articles.title: Zelda Live-Action Movie Moves Up to April 30, 2027
articles.summary: Nintendo has shifted The Legend of Zelda live-action movie one week earlier to April 30, 2027, after it had already been delayed from its original March 2027 date. Series creator Shigeru Miyamoto made the announcement personally, saying the team is working hard to get the film to audiences as soon as possible. Filming only wrapped in April 2026, so with less than a year to go, the hype is about to hit a new level.
articles.content:
Nintendo just moved The Legend of Zelda live-action movie up a week. It will now hit theaters worldwide on April 30, 2027, instead of May 7. The news came straight from Shigeru Miyamoto, the legendary creator of the Zelda franchise, who posted the update himself. As Miyamoto put it: "There's less than a year to go until release, so thank you for waiting."

This is a bit of a rollercoaster timeline. The Legend of Zelda movie was first announced in November 2023 and was originally set for March 2027. It then got pushed back to May 7, 2027. Now it is being pulled forward again, which is a rare and confident move from Nintendo. A company famous for delaying games is suddenly in a hurry.

The film is directed by Wes Ball, who made the Maze Runner trilogy and Kingdom of the Planet of the Apes. Benjamin Evan Ainsworth plays hero Link, and Bo Bragason plays Princess Zelda. Filming took place in New Zealand, in some of the same locations used for Peter Jackson's Lord of the Rings trilogy, and officially wrapped in April 2026. Nintendo is co-financing the project alongside Sony Pictures, with Nintendo holding more than 50 percent of the financial stake. After its theatrical run, the film will stream exclusively on Netflix.

No trailer has dropped yet, which means the next few months are going to feel like a long wait. But with the release date locked in and filming done, something has to show up soon. The Legend of Zelda is one of the most beloved video game franchises ever made, so when that first trailer finally lands, it will be a very big deal.
keyPoints: The Legend of Zelda live-action movie is now releasing April 30, 2027, one week earlier than planned
Shigeru Miyamoto announced the date change personally
The film was previously delayed from March 2027 to May 2027 before this new move
Filming wrapped in New Zealand in April 2026, and no trailer has been released yet
After its theatrical run the film will stream exclusively on Netflix

articles.title: The Strokes Drop 'Falling Out of Love' Before Colbert Live Debut
articles.summary: The Strokes have released 'Falling Out of Love', the second single from their upcoming album Reality Awaits, due June 26. Julian Casablancas and co. will give the song its live premiere on The Late Show with Stephen Colbert tonight, May 14, one of the show's final episodes before it wraps on May 21. The track runs over six minutes and features Casablancas singing through heavy Auto-Tune over a slow, groovy bassline from Nikolai Fraiture.
articles.content:
The Strokes are back with another taste of their long-awaited new album. The New York rock icons dropped 'Falling Out of Love' on Wednesday, May 13, the second single from Reality Awaits, which arrives June 26 via Cult/RCA. If you have been waiting six years for new Strokes music, the wait is nearly over.

Reality Awaits is the band's seventh album and their first since 2020's The New Abnormal. It was produced by Rick Rubin and recorded in Costa Rica before being finished across several international locations. The nine-song record has been one of the most anticipated rock releases in years, and 'Falling Out of Love' is the fourth track on its tracklist.

The song itself is a slow-burning, dreamy heartbreak ballad that clocks in at over six minutes, making it the longest track on the album. It is a big tonal shift from April's lead single 'Going Shopping', which had a more upbeat energy. Here, Julian Casablancas leans into tender acceptance, with a loping midtempo groove anchored by Nikolai Fraiture's bassline. Casablancas sings through heavy Auto-Tune throughout, a sonic choice that has become a defining feature of both Reality Awaits singles so far.

Tonight, Thursday May 14, The Strokes will give 'Falling Out of Love' its live debut on The Late Show with Stephen Colbert. It is a significant moment: Colbert's show ends for good on May 21, so this is one of its very last musical performances. After that, The Strokes kick off a major world tour starting June 12 at Bonnaroo in Manchester, Tennessee, hitting arenas and festivals across North America, South America, and Japan throughout the summer.
keyPoints: The Strokes released 'Falling Out of Love', the second single from upcoming album Reality Awaits
Reality Awaits drops June 26 and is the band's first album since 2020's The New Abnormal
The song is produced by Rick Rubin and runs over six minutes with heavy Auto-Tune on Julian Casablancas's vocals
The live debut happens tonight on The Late Show with Stephen Colbert, one of its last ever episodes
The Strokes launch a major world tour starting June 12 at Bonnaroo

articles.title: Camp Miasma Stuns Cannes, Earns 6-Minute Standing Ovation
articles.summary: Hannah Einbinder and Gillian Anderson star in Camp Miasma, a blood-soaked queer slasher comedy that had its world premiere in Cannes' Un Certain Regard section on Wednesday. The film left the audience equal parts baffled and thrilled, earning a massive 6-minute standing ovation. It is already one of the most talked-about films at the 79th Cannes Film Festival.
articles.content:
Camp Miasma just announced itself as the wildest thing at Cannes this year. The queer slasher comedy had its world premiere on Wednesday in the festival's Un Certain Regard section, a prestigious sidebar that spotlights bold and boundary-pushing cinema outside the main competition. And bold does not even begin to cover it.

Hannah Einbinder, best known for playing Ava on HBO's Hacks, and Gillian Anderson, the screen legend from The X-Files and The Crown, lead the cast. Both actresses reportedly appear blood-soaked on screen, and the film does not hold back on outrageous murders or explicit content. It is described as equal parts uproariously funny and confoundingly heady, which is a polite way of saying it will not be for everyone.

Cannes audiences, who are famously hard to impress, gave Camp Miasma a 6-minute standing ovation. That is a big deal. Ovation length at Cannes is treated almost like a sport, and anything over five minutes is considered a serious statement. The film clearly hit a nerve, even if it also left plenty of people confused by its more cerebral turns.

Un Certain Regard, for those unfamiliar, is Cannes' second-tier official selection. It has launched the careers of directors like Yorgos Lanthimos and Park Chan-wook, and it tends to favour the weird, the daring, and the genuinely new. Camp Miasma fits that tradition perfectly. Keep your eyes on this one as awards season approaches.
keyPoints: Camp Miasma is a queer slasher comedy starring Hannah Einbinder and Gillian Anderson
It premiered in Cannes' Un Certain Regard section on Wednesday 13 May
The film earned a 6-minute standing ovation despite also confounding some viewers
Both lead actresses appear blood-soaked on screen in what is described as uproariously funny and heady
Un Certain Regard is Cannes' prestigious sidebar for bold, boundary-pushing films

articles.title: Foo Fighters Squeeze All Six Members Into Their First Tiny Desk
articles.summary: Foo Fighters made their long-overdue debut on NPR's Tiny Desk Concert series, playing a five-song set that mixed hits with new material from their latest album Your Favorite Toy. The band performed classics like 'Everlong,' 'My Hero,' and 'Learn to Fly' alongside the new single 'Spit Shine.' Dave Grohl even revealed it was watching Trouble Funk pack into the tiny office space that made him finally want to do it.
articles.content:
Foo Fighters have been selling out stadiums for three decades, so watching all six of them squeeze behind NPR's little office desk is genuinely something to see. On May 13, 2026, the band made their first ever appearance on NPR's Tiny Desk Concert series, and they did not show up quietly.

NPR's Tiny Desk managed to accommodate all six Foo Fighters on the office visit, where the group played a five-song set of hits and their Your Favorite Toy single, 'Spit Shine,' which they opened with. From there, the band dipped into their back catalogue to play 'Learn to Fly,' 'My Hero,' and 'Everlong.' That is a tight and absolutely loaded setlist for any rock fan.

The prep work behind the scenes was no joke. According to the Tiny Desk team, Foo Fighters had been prepping for weeks, even going so far as to recreate the dimensions of the Tiny Desk space in their own practice studio to see how they could configure themselves properly. Apparently that all went out the window once Foo Fighters got to NPR's office, with frontman Dave Grohl joking, 'If you put instruments in our hands and there are people, it's fun to play!'

Grohl also got personal about why this gig meant so much to him. Before launching into 'Learn to Fly,' Grohl told the office how special the gig was, saying, 'It's an honor to be here.' Grohl also took a moment to mention two of his own favourite Tiny Desk Concerts, by Trouble Funk and Juvenile. Grohl credits seeing 12 members of Trouble Funk squeeze behind the desk as making him want to do it himself.

The Tiny Desk set comes as the band is deep into a massive 2026 cycle behind Your Favorite Toy, their 12th studio album. Other than an appearance at BottleRock Napa Valley, the next time North American fans will be able to see Foo Fighters is when they return for close to two months of dates in August, kicking things off at Toronto's Rogers Stadium. Going from a tiny NPR office to a full North American stadium run in the same year is a very Foo Fighters move.
keyPoints: Foo Fighters made their first ever NPR Tiny Desk Concert appearance on May 13, 2026
All six band members crammed into the NPR office for a five-song set
Setlist included 'Spit Shine' from new album Your Favorite Toy plus classics 'Everlong,' 'My Hero,' and 'Learn to Fly'
The band rehearsed in a replica of the Tiny Desk space for weeks beforehand
Dave Grohl said watching Trouble Funk at the Tiny Desk inspired him to finally do it

articles.title: Musk Says Nolan Cast Lupita Nyong'o for Awards, Not The Odyssey
articles.summary: Elon Musk has renewed his attacks on Christopher Nolan's upcoming film The Odyssey, claiming on X that Nolan cast Lupita Nyong'o as Helen of Troy purely because 'he wants the awards.' The backlash intensified after a May 12 Time magazine profile confirmed Nyong'o not only plays Helen of Troy but also her sister Clytemnestra — a double role nobody saw coming. The $250 million epic hits cinemas on July 17, and the controversy is already making it one of the most talked-about films of the summer.
articles.content:
Elon Musk is back on his Odyssey soapbox, and this time the confirmation of Lupita Nyong'o's role gave him more to work with. A Time magazine cover story published May 12 officially confirmed that Nyong'o will play Helen of Troy in Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey. That alone would have set the internet on fire. But the profile also revealed she has a second role in the film as Clytemnestra, Helen's sister and the wife of Agamemnon. Two roles. One actress. Cue the meltdown.

Musk's response on X was swift and blunt. When a user asked why Nolan and other directors "race swap white characters," Musk replied that it comes down to "quotas and awards" before adding in a separate post that Nolan simply "wants the awards." He was referencing the Academy's representation and inclusion standards for Best Picture eligibility, which were established in 2020. But here is the catch: those standards cover a combination of casting, crew, leadership, apprenticeships, and audience development. A film with an all-white cast can still meet the criteria in other ways. In fact, Nolan's own Oppenheimer had an all-white cast and took home seven Oscars, including Best Picture and Best Director.

Musk did not stop at Nyong'o. He also amplified posts mocking Elliot Page, who previously worked with Nolan on Inception, amid unconfirmed rumors that Page could play Achilles in The Odyssey. Musk called the idea "one of the dumbest and twisted things I've ever heard" and laughed at an AI-generated photo of Page in Greek armor struggling to open a pickle jar. He also agreed with a user who called Nolan "racist against the Greek people and their cultural heritage," simply replying: "True."

The backlash has not gone unanswered. Whoopi Goldberg addressed the controversy on The View, pointing out that Nyong'o is widely regarded as one of the world's most beautiful women. Meanwhile, The Odyssey's enormous cast, which includes Matt Damon as Odysseus, Tom Holland as Telemachus, Anne Hathaway as Penelope, Zendaya as Athena, and Robert Pattinson as Antinous, has barely been mentioned in all the noise. Musk has been sounding off about this film since February, when Nyong'o's casting was still just a rumor and he said it would be an "insult" to Homer. Now that it is official, it seems the volume is only going up before The Odyssey opens on July 17.
keyPoints: Elon Musk claimed Christopher Nolan cast Lupita Nyong'o as Helen of Troy to win Oscars, writing 'He wants the awards' on X.
A May 12 Time magazine profile confirmed Nyong'o plays both Helen of Troy and her sister Clytemnestra in the $250 million film.
Musk's Oscar quota argument falls flat — Nolan's Oppenheimer had an all-white cast and won seven Oscars including Best Picture.
Musk also mocked Elliot Page over unconfirmed rumors of a role as Achilles, calling the idea 'one of the dumbest and twisted things I've ever heard.'
The Odyssey opens in cinemas on July 17 with a massive cast including Matt Damon, Tom Holland, Anne Hathaway, and Zendaya.

articles.title: Trump Claims a New Drug Brings Dead People Back to Life
articles.summary: Donald Trump told a press event on May 11 that a new drug can bring people back from the dead, describing a patient who received last rites before making a full recovery. The unnamed drug appears to be connected to Trump's recent push around psychedelic treatments, including ibogaine. The claim has gone viral and left a lot of people with a lot of questions.
articles.content:
Donald Trump made a statement on Monday, May 11 that would have stopped the world if it came from a doctor or a scientist. Standing at a routine press event, Trump declared that a new drug can bring dead people back to life.

During what started as a routine press event, Trump casually made an announcement that in any normal White House would have kicked off days of frenzied coverage, either about a revelatory inflection point in the history of medicine, or about the mental wellness of the country's chief executive. His exact words, "We've taken people that were dead. We had a person given the last rites — gone, the kids are crying and everything — and started them on this drug. And the person became better. It works."

Trump did not name the drug. But the comments came in the context of his administration's recent push around psychedelic medicine. Trump recently signed an Executive Order accelerating research and improving access to psychedelic drugs as promising treatments for serious mental illnesses, prioritizing breakthroughs that could offer new hope to millions of Americans, particularly veterans who face disproportionately high rates of suicide and often don't respond to conventional therapies. One drug at the center of that push is ibogaine. Ibogaine is a naturally occurring compound from a shrub native to Africa, used to treat depression, anxiety, addiction, post-traumatic stress disorder and brain trauma. Because it is illegal in the United States, Americans have been traveling to unregulated clinics, often in Mexico or the Caribbean, to take it.

To be clear, no drug that raises the dead exists. What likely happened is that a critically ill patient, perhaps one given very low odds of survival, recovered after being given an experimental treatment. Trump's retelling turned that into something far more dramatic. The internet reacted as you would expect, with the clip going wide fast and commentators from all sides weighing in on what exactly the president thought he was describing. Whether this was a misunderstanding, a colorful exaggeration, or something else entirely, the quote is now out there and it is not going away anytime soon.
keyPoints: Trump claimed on May 11 that a new drug can bring dead people back to life
He described a patient who received last rites and then recovered after taking the drug
Trump did not name the specific drug in his remarks
The comments appear tied to his administration's recent executive order fast-tracking psychedelic drug research, including ibogaine
No drug that reverses death exists; experts believe Trump dramatically overstated a patient recovery story

articles.title: Westminster Dog Show Leaves Fox for Netflix in 2027
articles.summary: The Westminster Kennel Club Dog Show is leaving Fox Sports and heading to Netflix, where it will stream live for the first time starting in February 2027. The 151st edition of America's second-longest continuously held sporting event will feature over 3,000 dogs from 200 breeds, with Best in Show crowning at Madison Square Garden. It is a major live-sports win for Netflix as it continues to build out its sports programming.
articles.content:
One of America's most beloved annual spectacles just got a new home. The Westminster Kennel Club Dog Show will move from Fox Sports to Netflix starting next year under a new deal. Fox Sports has aired the main show events since 2017. Now Netflix is taking over, and it is a big moment for the streamer's growing live-sports ambitions.

The 151st Westminster Kennel Club Dog Show takes place January 30 through February 2, 2027, with daytime breed and agility events held at the Jacob K. Javits Convention Center and evening group judging and Best in Show hosted at Madison Square Garden. Daytime breed competitions will be streamed live simultaneously on Tudum.com and westminsterkennelclub.org, while evening judging at Madison Square Garden will be live on Netflix proper on February 1 and 2.

The three-day, three-night event will feature over 3,000 champion dogs representing 200 breeds from around the world, all culminating in the coveted Best in Show title on February 2, 2027, at Madison Square Garden. Marking his fourth judging assignment at Westminster, Espen Engh, a third-generation Greyhound fancier, top champion breeder, world-renowned judge, and veterinarian by education, will serve as Best in Show judge. With four decades of judging experience spanning over 100 countries, Engh is the only active judge to receive an invitation to award Best in Show at both Westminster and Crufts. That is serious dog-world royalty.

The Westminster Kennel Club Dog Show is the second-longest continuously held sporting event in the United States, following the Kentucky Derby, and the longest nationally televised live dog show since 1948. Westminster president Dr. Donald Sturz said the Netflix deal is about growth, noting that bringing the Westminster Dog Show to Netflix not only expands the reach of the event but also introduces the sport of dogs to new audiences around the world. Netflix vice president of nonfiction series and sports Brandon Riegg added that as a proud dog owner himself, it is a real pleasure and honor to partner with The Westminster Kennel Club to air its 151st show live around the world on Netflix.
keyPoints: The Westminster Kennel Club Dog Show is leaving Fox Sports for Netflix starting in February 2027
The 151st show runs January 30 to February 2, with Best in Show at Madison Square Garden on February 2
Over 3,000 dogs from 200 breeds will compete across three days and three nights
Norwegian judge Espen Engh will preside over Best in Show — the only active judge invited to do so at both Westminster and Crufts
Daytime events stream on Tudum and westminsterkennelclub.org; evening judging is live on Netflix

articles.title: Man Earns $746K Working 5 Remote Jobs at Once and Feels Guilty About It
articles.summary: A millennial in IT is secretly juggling five remote jobs at the same time, pulling in about $746,000 a year while still logging off by 5pm every day. He told Business Insider the wave of tech layoffs has made him feel guilty about the practice, known as overemployment. His story has reignited a debate about loyalty, remote work, and who the real rule-breakers are in the modern workplace.
articles.content:
Picture this. You are on a video call for one job, answering Slack messages for another, and running automated scripts for a third. Meanwhile it is not even lunchtime. This is the daily reality for one millennial IT worker who is secretly holding down five remote jobs simultaneously and taking home around $746,000 a year for the effort.

The practice has a name. Overemployment is the growing trend of remote workers quietly holding multiple full-time jobs without telling any of their employers. It exploded in popularity during the remote work boom of the early 2020s, with entire online communities forming around tips for juggling meetings, hiding software activity, and managing conflicting deadlines. The logic is simple. If your employer can offshore your role or cut you without warning, why show the same loyalty in return?

But this particular worker is feeling conflicted. He told Business Insider that the recent wave of tech industry layoffs has triggered genuine guilt about what he is doing. Tech has seen massive rounds of job cuts over the past few years, with hundreds of thousands of workers losing their positions at companies of all sizes. Watching colleagues get let go while he quietly collects five salaries has hit differently.

He still clocks out by 5pm every day. That detail is doing a lot of work in this story. It suggests that at least in his case, five jobs do not require five times the effort. Automation, efficient workflows, and jobs that are simply not as demanding as their salary suggests all play a role. The story raises uncomfortable questions about the nature of white-collar work, the productivity expectations baked into IT salaries, and whether a single employer's claim on your entire working day was ever really justified. There are no easy answers here, but the conversation is not going away anytime soon.
keyPoints: A millennial is secretly working five remote IT jobs at once, earning about $746,000 a year
He still finishes work by 5pm each day despite holding all five positions
The practice is called overemployment and has grown significantly since the remote work boom
Recent tech industry layoffs have made him feel guilty about doing it
His story has reignited debate about employer loyalty and the nature of remote work

articles.title: Polymarket Bettors Think Nate Jacobs Dies in Euphoria Season 3
articles.summary: Prediction market Polymarket is hosting a live betting contract on which Euphoria Season 3 characters will die, with Nate Jacobs and Rue Bennett currently leading the odds. Some bettors appear to be winning suspiciously large sums, raising real questions about whether a few people have insider knowledge. The market resolves May 31, the same day as the season finale.
articles.content:
Euphoria is HBO's hit drama about the chaotic, often brutal lives of young people navigating addiction, love, and identity. It stars Zendaya as Rue Bennett, a teenager in and out of recovery, and Jacob Elordi as Nate Jacobs, the show's most volatile antagonist. Season 3 is the final run, and with that comes the question every fan is screaming: who actually dies?

Polymarket, a prediction platform where users bet real money on real-world outcomes, has turned that question into a live trading market. The contract is called "Who will die in Euphoria: Season 3?" and right now, the numbers are striking. Nate Jacobs sits at an 82% implied chance of dying this season, while Rue Bennett, the lead character played by Zendaya, is at 61%. These are not casual fan theories. These are priced bets with real money behind them.

The story gets more interesting when you zoom out. Fast Company reported that some of the biggest winners on Polymarket entertainment markets do not seem to be getting lucky. A handful of users are racking up outsized wins in ways that feel a little too consistent to be coincidence. The obvious question: are some of these bettors actually on set, connected to production, or tipped off somehow? Polymarket's rules say a death must happen on screen or be explicitly confirmed in the show to count, so vague spoilers alone would not be enough. But someone with real inside information could absolutely clean up here.

The market has been updating in real time with every new episode. Episode 5, which aired May 11, ended on a cliffhanger that left at least one main character in serious danger, described as facing peril in a ditch. That moment sent betting volumes surging. The season finale airs May 31, which is also the resolution date for the Polymarket contract. Whoever is holding the right positions that night stands to win big. And whether that is skill, luck, or a leaked script, nobody outside of a few wallets truly knows.
keyPoints: Nate Jacobs is trading at 82% odds of dying in Euphoria Season 3 on Polymarket
Rue Bennett, played by Zendaya, is at 61% odds of dying
The market resolves on May 31, the same day as the season finale
Some unusually large winners are raising suspicion about possible insider knowledge
A qualifying death must happen on screen or be explicitly confirmed in the show

articles.title: YouTube Brandcast: Chappell Roan and Zara Larsson Close a Big Night
articles.summary: YouTube's annual Brandcast upfront took over Lincoln Center on May 13, with Trevor Noah hosting and both Zara Larsson and Chappell Roan performing live. The night was YouTube's boldest pitch yet to advertisers, making the case that it has already won the streaming war. YouTube CEO Neal Mohan declared it "the YouTube era" and backed it up with a stacked creator slate and some eye-popping audience numbers.
articles.content:
YouTube does not do things quietly at Upfronts week. On Wednesday evening at Lincoln Center in New York City, YouTube held its annual Brandcast event, and it came loaded. YouTube, which has been increasingly competing with the likes of Disney and Netflix at the upfronts, went big with its opening: a performance by Zara Larsson, the Swedish pop star who is having a moment.

Trevor Noah, former host of Comedy Central's The Daily Show, was the MC for the night. Noah has since made the jump to YouTube creator full-time, and he leaned into that transition with humor. "I would run into my own family members, like 'Why do I know you, from YouTube?'" he joked. Noah also got a few laughs with a reference to the classic YouTube video "Charlie Bit My Finger." Noah shed the tag of late-night host when he abruptly left The Daily Show in 2022, and he is now hosting Trevor Noah's World Tour for YouTube, a travel series where he spotlights local people, food, and culture.

"Welcome to the YouTube era," YouTube CEO Neal Mohan declared after stepping on stage following Larsson's opening performance. Mohan's pitch to advertisers was direct. Per Nielsen, YouTube has been the number one platform in watch time for three years running, and the company says it reached 244 million viewers aged 18 and up in November, roughly 91% of the U.S. adult population. YouTube has also paid out more than $100 billion to creators, artists, and media companies over the past four years.

The creator slate announced for YouTube was genuinely impressive. The roster includes Kareem Rahma's Keep the Meter Running, a series in which the comedian rides along with yellow cab drivers, Alex Cooper's four-title slate from her Unwell banner, and Trevor Noah globetrotting in Trevor Noah's World Tour. Cleo Abram, Johnny Harris, Morgan Jay, HopeScope, and Julian Shapiro-Barnum, who is launching a weekly late-night show called Outside Tonight, rounded out the slate.

YouTube ended Brandcast with a set from Chappell Roan, and it had some extra muscle behind it: she was creating for YouTube years before she became famous. Grammy-winner Chappell Roan, who has 2.4 million YouTube followers, took the stage as the headline performer. According to YouTube, the platform "offered an early launchpad and discovery engine for her music," including her NPR Tiny Desk and Coachella performances. As a closing argument that YouTube makes stars, it was hard to argue with.
keyPoints: YouTube held its 2026 Brandcast upfront at Lincoln Center on May 13 with Trevor Noah as host
Zara Larsson opened the show and Chappell Roan closed it with live performances
YouTube CEO Neal Mohan declared it 'the YouTube era' and cited 244 million monthly U.S. viewers
YouTube has paid out over $100 billion to creators over the past four years
New creator shows from Kareem Rahma, Alex Cooper, Cleo Abram, and Julian Shapiro-Barnum were announced

articles.title: Foo Fighters Play Their First-Ever Tiny Desk With Everlong and My Hero
articles.summary: Foo Fighters finally showed up to NPR's Tiny Desk Concert series, squeezing all six members into the office space for a five-song set. They opened with 'Spit Shine' from their new album Your Favorite Toy and closed with beloved classics 'My Hero' and 'Everlong'. Dave Grohl said seeing Trouble Funk pack the space inspired him to finally make it happen.
articles.content:
After three decades as one of rock's biggest bands, Foo Fighters finally did the thing. All six members crammed into NPR's tiny Washington D.C. office and played one of the most intimate sets of their career. It was their first-ever Tiny Desk Concert, and by all accounts, it delivered.

The band played a five-song set that opened with 'Spit Shine,' a single from their 12th album Your Favorite Toy, before diving deep into the back catalog. They rolled through 'Learn to Fly,' 'My Hero,' and closed with 'Everlong,' two of the most recognizable rock songs of the last 30 years. Getting that kind of energy into a cramped office setting is no small feat, and Foo Fighters pulled it off.

The preparation was serious. According to the Tiny Desk team, Foo Fighters actually taped out the dimensions of the Tiny Desk space in their own rehearsal studio to figure out how to physically fit six people and all their gear. Dave Grohl joked that once the instruments came out, none of that planning really mattered anyway. He told the crowd, "If you put instruments in our hands and there are people, it's fun to play!"

Grohl also got sentimental before launching into 'Learn to Fly,' calling the gig an honor and crediting a specific past Tiny Desk performance for inspiring him. "I have to say, when I saw Trouble Funk squeeze into this tiny little office space here, I was like, 'It's not that tiny,'" he said. The full performance is streaming now on YouTube. For fans who want a bigger dose, Foo Fighters kick off a North American stadium tour in August, starting at Toronto's Rogers Stadium.
keyPoints: Foo Fighters played their first-ever NPR Tiny Desk Concert, featuring all six band members
The five-song set opened with 'Spit Shine' from album Your Favorite Toy
Classics 'Everlong', 'My Hero', and 'Learn to Fly' were also performed
The band recreated the Tiny Desk dimensions in their rehearsal space to prep
Their North American stadium tour kicks off in August at Toronto's Rogers Stadium
